STATEMENT OF ITEMS IN DISPUTE RE INTERROGATORIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 100005863) FILED BY DEFENDANT ROMANSKI, DANIEL
DECLARATION OF DANIEL P. IANNTELLI IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 100005863) FILED BY DEFENDANT ROMANSKI, DANIEL

LAW AND MOTION, 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT WORKERS COMPENSATION SOLUTIONS, L.L.C'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD, FOURTH, FIFTH, SIXTH, SEVENTH, EIGHT, NINTH, TENTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF DANIEL ROMANSKI'S CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT CORRECTLY POINTS OUT THAT CALIFORNIA LAW PERMITS PLEADING INCONSISTENT THEORIES OF LIABILITY. AS CROSS-COMPLAINANT NOTES, "[O]NE OF THE TYPICAL OCCASIONS FOR ALTERNATIVE PLEADING IS UNCERTAINTY ABOUT THE NATURE OF A CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATION THAT THE EVIDENCE MAY ESTABLISH: EXPRESS, IMPLIED IN FACT, OR QUASI-CONTRACTUAL." (4 WITKIN, CAL. PROCEDURE (5TH ED. 2008) PLEADING, 407.) CROSS-P BELIEVES THAT IT WILL BE ABLE TO BRING A SUCCESSFUL BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT FOR CROSS-DEFENDANT'S BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. HOWEVER, IF FACTS EMERGE WHICH INDICATE THAT THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS NOT ENFORCEABLE, CROSS-PLAINTIFF IS ALLOWED TO ASSERT THE UNDERLYING CAUSES OF ACTIONS AS AN ALTERNATIVE MEANS OF ESTABLISHING LIABILITY. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON VILLAGE NORTHRIDGE IS MISPLACED BECAUSE CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS NOT ALLEGING A CLAIM OF FRAUD IN THE INDUCEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/AJR

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF WORKERS COMPENSATION SOLUTIONS, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS ON ALL OF THE CLAIMS ALLEGED IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. NONE OF THE CLAIMS ALLEGED IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT SHOW ON THEIR FACE THAT THE CLAIM IS BASED ENTIRELY ON EVENTS THAT OCCURRED PRIOR TO THE DATE THAT MR. ROMANSKI FILED HIS BANKRUPTCY PETITION. THEREFORE, THE COURT CANNOT SAY THAT MR. ROMANSKI EITHER LACKS STANDING TO ASSERT ANY OF THOSE CLAIMS OR THAT HE IS ESTOPPED FROM PURSUING ANY OF THOSE CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-19-2016 Continued To Jul-18-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiff And Cross Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Trial Points And Authorities, Supporting Declaration
Off Calendar May-19-2016 Text Ruling

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Apr-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF WORKERS COMPENSATION SOLUTIONS, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS on all of the claims alleged in the cross-complaint is denied. None of the claims alleged in the cross-complaint show on their face that the claim is based entirely on events that occurred prior to the date that Mr. Romanski filed his bankruptcy petition. Therefore, the court cannot say that Mr. Romanski either lacks standing to assert any of those claims or that he is estopped from pursuing any of those claims. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-23-2015 Continued To Nov-18-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ARTHUR A. ZORIO OF THE BROWNSTEIN, HYATT FARBER & SCHRECK LLP, TEL. NO. 1-775-324-4100 EXT. 112 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. RYAN T. DUNN OF DUNN & PANAGOTACOS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-982-2100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT WORKERS COMPENSATION SOLUTIONS, L.L.C'S DEMURRER TO the third, fourth, fifth, sixth, seventh, eight, ninth, tenth and eleventh causes of action of Daniel Romanski's CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. Cross-Complainant correctly points out that California law permits pleading inconsistent theories of liability. As Cross-complainant notes, "[o]ne of the typical occasions for alternative pleading is uncertainty about the nature of a contractual obligation that the evidence may establish: Express, implied in fact, or quasi-contractual." (4 Witkin, Cal. Procedure (5th Ed. 2008) Pleading, 407.) Cross-P believes that it will be able to bring a successful breach of contract claim against Cross-Defendant for Cross-Defendant's breach of the settlement agreement. However, if facts emerge which indicate that the settlement agreement is not enforceable, Cross-Plaintiff is allowed to assert the underlying causes of actions as an alternative means of establishing liability. Cross-Defendant's reliance on Village Northridge is misplaced because Cross-Complainant is not alleging a claim of fraud in the inducement. Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr
